summary: Excellent audience recording of a solid 1993 show with a standout Uncle John’s jam and soaring Space.
review: The three reviewers agree this is an unusually high‑quality audience capture from the early ’90s, noting the Neumann KM140 mic, Aerco preamp and Sony DAT decks that give it a clear, dreamy sound. Musically the concert is described as solid throughout, with a first set that flies by and a second set that shines thanks to a majestic Uncle John’s Band jam, an ear‑candy Space, and strong performances of Way to Go Home and Easy Answers.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews give 4‑star ratings, consistently praise both the performance (especially the second set) and the audience recording quality, yielding a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded with Neumann KM140 mic into an Aerco preamp; source likely Sony D‑10 Pro or SBM‑1 → D‑8 DAT deck. Reviewers describe the sound as clear, dreamy and one of the better post‑91 audience captures.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid first set that flies by, followed by an even stronger second set featuring a pre‑drums section and a majestic Uncle John’s Band jam, giving the concert a satisfying arc.
